full_text:
UPD 6.5.2026 JJ

ONBOARDING

Welcome to Färkkilä Lab! ✨

In this document you can find general information and a collection of links to our guidelines to help you start in the lab. There is a separate Oncosys onboarding session each month held by Karen Ahlnas, make sure you attend this once as well! Read more about our Mission, Values and Strategy:

Lab_values_mission_and_strategy

Lab Mission and Values

Table of Contents

Contact information

Principal Investigator

Anniina Färkkilä +358505967786, anniina.farkkila@helsinki.fi

Staff scientists contact information and their roles

Research Coordinator Joonas Jukonen - tel. 050 5748092, joonas.jukonen@helsinki.fi

Spatial Profiling Manager Saundarya Shah - tel. 0453474947, saundarya.shah@helsinki.fi

Laboratory coordinator: Foteini Chamchougia substituting for Anastasia Lundgren - tel. 0440526706, anastasiya.chernenko@helsinki.fi, NOTE! on maternity leave 10/2025–09/2026

IT Specialist: Debashish Deb -tel 0451600007, debashish.deb@helsinki.fi

Clinical team coordinator: Maija Vääriskoski - tel. 0453173171, maija.vaariskoski@helsinki.fi

Lab members phone numbers for emergencies:

Add your phone number: Lab_personnel_phone_numbers

GENERAL GUIDELINES

Meetings

Lab meetings (Research coordinator)

Lab meeting is once a week for 1.5hours. Schedule can be found here: Lab Meetings 2026 It is recommended to be on site unless you have a pressing need to be elsewhere. The meeting timeslot and location varies, details are found in the lab google calendar and the google sheet linked above. For pressing reasons, if you are unable to attend - mark it in the lab meeting schedule (Lab Meetings 2026, column “Unable to attend”) as soon as you know. If you attend the lab meeting remote, also mark your remote status to Lab Meetings 2026, under the column “Remote” so the group knows of the need to setup the remote connections. The Zoom link for the lab meetings can be found in the Google Calendar, lab meeting agenda, and the schedule.

In the beginning of each Lab meeting we will have a 30 minutes General lab meeting with a preplanned Agenda, which can be found here: Farkkilab meeting general agendas and notes. If you have suggestions that you would like to discuss, ask Joonas to add it to the agenda.

The Lab meeting program is coordinated by Research Coordinator (Joonas). It consists of one or two 30 minutes slots including the presentation and discussion. The schedule for the presentations can be found at Lab Meetings 2026. Lab meeting presentations are meant to be discussive, and allow for getting insights from the lab members to your project. It is especially encouraged to present project plans, concepts, technologies, ideas, challenges, preliminary data etc. which can benefit from feedback and not just fully ready results or projects.

The lab meeting slots are allocated during the end of the previous season, and loosely follow the presentation order of the previous season. The aim is for every member to present at least once per season. If you need to change your slot, contact Joonas - always as soon as possible.

Hosting Journal clubs Reviewer Club (TRC)

We use every second lab meeting to review relevant articles. There is always a host appointed who selects an article and leads the discussion during this meeting. For further information on how to host see the following document: Guidelines how to host a Third Reviewer Club or TRC

Infrastructure (IT) meetings (IT specialist)

The IT meeting, coordinated by Deb, is held on an as-needed basis every 1-2 months. The meeting focuses on data and project organisation and management.

Meeting is held as a working lunch serving pizza at 12-13, and EVERYONE should attend.

The meeting also instructs how to use different resources including CSC services for our data analysis workflows and identifying opportunities to further improve our research pipelines. All computational updates, issues, and proposed enhancements are shared and discussed to support continuous development of our computational processes.

A dedicated Slack channel #IT-meeting is available for ongoing discussions, meeting announcements, and follow-up communication related to IT and computational topics.

Cyclops meetings (spatial biology manager)

CyclOps (Cyclic operatives) meeting is hosted by Saun. It is a once a month meeting that includes discussion and suggestions to improve our tCycIF, GeoMX and Xenium operations. It is recommended for all the lab members who have planned or are planning to do tCycIF, GeoMX,Xenium experiments in the future. We also give updates on new methods, new validated antibodies, new instrument options available for these experiments. We have a dedicated #cyclops-meeting channel in slack for discussions and announcements about the meeting.

Computational troubleshooting (CT) meetings (Sara Palomino)

The CT meeting is hosted by Sara. It is a weekly, on-demand meeting aimed at discussing everyday computational challenges we encounter in our projects — for example, a script with persistent errors, a plot we are not satisfied with, an incompatible library, or any other issue we have been struggling to solve.

The meeting is also a space to share and discuss new computational tools, packages, or workflows that could benefit our ongoing analyses. Although the CT meeting is mainly recommended for computationally oriented members, all lab members are welcome to join and contribute.

Each week, we ask if anyone would like to present or bring a topic for discussion, and the meeting is scheduled accordingly. We also have a dedicated Slack channel, #computational_troubleshooting, for sharing announcements, materials, and follow-up discussions related to the meeting.

SOP for meetings

Booking a meeting with Anniina

Book in advance

It is easiest to find free slots within 1-2 weeks

It is encouraged to book the next meeting in the end of the previous meeting

Check calendars

Lab common and Anniina’s own for tentative slots

Anniina’s typical working hours are from 9-17 - if needed you can ask for exceptions

No more than 6 meetings per day - if urgent you can ask for exceptions

The max duration of effective meetings is 55min (1h slot) - ask personally for exceptions. Confirm the slot with Anniina using Slack private message.

General tips for meetings

Mark in the calendar what the topic is and who is joining. Please use complete words/sentences that describe the goal of the meeting - this makes it possible for everyone to prepare For example - NNN project meeting to check results of XXX and decide YYYY- Anniina, Ada, Joonas

To set up doodles for e.g. Thesis committee book tentative slots from Anniina’s and then generate the doodle

No more than 1x1h tentative slots per day

Spread the the slots over min 3 days and 2 weeks - this allows more flexibility for busy PIs

After the doodle is finalised, remove the tentative slots

You chair the meeting - here are some guidelines

Prepare agenda and lead the discussion - make sure you track the time

First 5-10 minutes is used to summarise the big picture, overall goals of the meeting and present the agenda

Last 5-10 minutes is used to

recap the conclusions

make sure everyone is aligned with the action items and their own tasks

book the next meeting if needed

Principles of project organisation in the lab

For tracking projects in the lab, we are using Project recording and Logs in GD. Create a folder in The Lab’s GDrive where you will store all the important information related to your project. Follow the next action points related to it: FAIR project organization action points

Wellbeing matters

General work wellbeing is coordinated by university HR and your main supervisor(s). If you have issues affecting your ability to work, first contact your official supervisor - usually Anniina.

For additional support for students struggling with e.g., internal or external expectations or the uncertainty they are facing at any step of their project, we have a dedicated Senior Scientist team (Hanna Elomaa, Joonas Jukonen, and Sara Palomino), who can be approached at a low threshold for personal support. These discussions are strictly confidential. The Senior Scientist team also has allotted timeslots in the lab calendar for these sessions, which can be booked by sending e.g. a private message to any of the Senior Scientist team members in Slack. See also One-on one support meetings for students for more info about the support meetings. These meetings are intended to be a first-line assistance for when you are starting to notice issues in your wellbeing, and you want to discuss informally with people who have been through similar experiences.

If you feel that you need more support than what we can offer in-house, the occupational healthcare provided for salaried personnel by Mehiläinen, and YTHS for basic degree students has mental health resources available (https://www.mehilainen.fi/en/occupational-health, https://www.yths.fi/en/frontpage/)

Key card/flexim (Research coordinator)

Ask Karen Ahlnäs to handle it for you - if Karen is unavailable, do the following:

Send an email to anne.simola@helsinki.fi

Instruction: https://biomedicumfi.sharepoint.com/SitePages/Keys,-Security-Surveillance,-Identification-Card.aspx

Go to the security center to take a photo for a photo ID (if the contract is > 3 months)

Collect the ready keys from the info desk

HR specialist contact in the Faculty of Medicine

Päivi Sinisalo - paivi.sinisalo@helsinki.fi, phone: 029412411, 0503185426
Päivi can help you with HR related issues, for example your contract, from the university side.

General information for international lab members: Tips for international members

Holidays and sick leave

You can find detailed information about how to mark holidays and sick leaves in the ONCOSYS orientation document.

For holidays you can find the internal guidelines here, please check these and follow the instructions when planning your holidays: Holidays

Remember to mark your sick leave days in the lab calendar as well. You can find additional information about marking sick leave at the following wiki page: How to mark your sick leave

Travelling and working remotely

For work related travel (eg. conferences) you can find information in the ONCOSYS orientation document and in Flamma (https://flamma.helsinki.fi/en/group/matkustaminen). 

For remote working instructions, see Working remotely

Short personal absences within a working day - not holiday/work related

If you need to be unavailable due to a personal matter (e.g. for a doctor’s/bank appointment) for a short 1-2 hour period within your working day, you can use the marking Away on a Personal Matters (APM) in the lab Google calendar (e.g. Anniina-APM).

You can but you don't have to disclose the exact reason for being away.

Please ensure the APM booking accounts for the whole time of the event, including travel.

DRY LAB

Every new person in the lab will receive a general tour and be introduced to lab practices by the lab coordinator.

Spaces

Anniina’s office- B327b

Minion’s office - B323b

Queendom - B327a

Vähärautio lab office (the lab coordinator (Aino’s / Anastasia’s) sits here) - B317a

Seating plan: Lab desks

For full-time members, we allocate permanent spots

For people who are part-time and do not spend much time in the lab, we have allocated desks which work in a first-come-first-served basis

On particularly busy days if there is no desk available, you can request a place from the overflow office. This is located on the same floor and corridor.

Accounts

You will need to gain access to several accounts including internal communication channels and data storage, your tutor will help with this.

University of Helsinki account

grp-farkkila mailing list: Ask Joonas to add you

Slack (Färkkilä Lab and Oncosys workspaces, Joonas can add you to both)

Oncosys-OVA Teams environment – Maija can add you to the environment

Github - https://github.com/farkkilab

Google Calendar

Färkkilä Lab (Joonas/Saun can add you)

Anniina’s calendar (Joonas/Saun can add you)

Lola’s calendar (for t-CycIF project planning - responsible person Saun) also #management-spatialprofiling in Slack

Eduuni - you will be granted access only based on need of clinical data (responsible person Maija)

Quartzy for wet lab people ordering reagents,buffers,chemicals,antibodies (responsible person: Saun , ask her to be added to quartzy)

Wiki:

Färkkilä lab wiki is being phased out in favor for Google Drive, but still contains general instructions

Färkkilä – Joonas can add you if your tutor can not

ONCOSYS-Ova wiki is for the whole consortium, there we have Samples info table and Instructions for sample collectors and users

Resources

Laptops

Either borrowed from the University or paid by the lab - ask Joonas about getting a laptop if you need one

Laptop and external drive inventory: IT_inventory_computers_and_drives

Workstations - Find all information and responsible people in Google Drive. They are meant to be common resources for heavy computational work. Pick the most suitable resource for your project and communicate the usage in Slack use the channel: #csc-pouta-and-workstations-users.

Usage guidelines for workstations

CSC

CSC_resources

Storage

Keep your data FAIR - Findable, Accessible, Interoperable, Reusable

Guidelines to organise and clean your data: Guidelines about data organisation

Hard discs

Inventory: IT_inventory_computers_and_drives

Network drives and datacloud

Network_drives_and_datacloud

Allas - CSC

CSC_resources

General data guidelines

It is important that all results and data produced in our Lab follow the FAIR principles. Here is more information about it:
FAIR data and documentation 12022025

Be mindful about sensitive data and store it at the correct storage places!

Clinical data handling

Do regular backups!

Clean your data, you can find the guidelines in section Resources - Storage

You are responsible for your own data, add clear READMEs to your folders

If you are doing CyCIF, join the Slack channels #spatial-experimental and #spatialprofiling_management

Everything related to it is posted and pinned in this channel

Lab subscriptions

We have learning resources in the lab for everyone’s usage.

ChatGPT Pro - https://chatgpt.com/

When using the lab account, generate a project for you/topic, and keep all related chats in this project folder - this way we can keep ChatGPT organised and people can easily access their previous searches.

DataCamp - https://www.datacamp.com/

Note that for the certification diplomas, only the name and email of the Lab account will appear, but you can still learn so much from the platform.

Lab social media

We are trying to create a presence in the online community and communicate our science through these channels. If you are uncomfortable to appear in social media posts, let us know, it is completely fine and respected! The responsible person for this is Maria.

Webpage: https://farkkilab.org/

To add publications to the website’s collection, reach out to Saun

For the rest of the content, Ada is the admin:

All communications are handled in the #update-webpage Slack channel, do not contact her individually for website-related issues.

When you join, share asap a message with your full name, programme or position description (eg. TRANSMED MSc student, Post-Doc…) , a picture and description. You can get inspiration from the ones in the lab-members space.

If you wish to modify something from the website, you can also communicate in that Slack channel the things you wish to change.

There are periodic updates so you might need to wait until the next one for the information to be modified

Bluesky: https://bsky.app/profile/farkkilab.bsky.social

Lab credit card orders

Read first: How to use the lab credit card

After completing the purchase, send by email the following information immediately to Saun. (If you have doubts about WBS , please ask Joonas about which WBS to use):

Invoice or receipt - needs to contain VAT % information

Hotel, airline booking confirmation

WBS to be used

Food order/Restaurant/Alcohol:

List of participants (names)

Occasion

In case of paper receipt immediately give it to Saun

Make sure Saun is aware of the receipt if you leave it on her desk or the receipt box above her desk

More details: Credit card purchase invoicing information

WET LAB

To every new wet lab member: Onboarding info & tour around the facilities with the lab coordinator.

General wet lab practices

Safety and protective equipment

Safety comes first. Protective equipment is available and must be used when handling hazardous chemicals or biological materials. This includes gloves, lab coat, eye protection, goggles, an apron, cryoprotective gloves, and face mask when needed.

You need to know when to use gloves, a lab coat, and other protective gear, when to work in the fume hood, and when sterile work in the laminar hood is required. Remember to switch the fume hood on before use.

If you do not know where to find safety items, ask people in the lab. More information about safety equipment can be found in the ONCOSYS orientation document: 2021_ONCOSYS_Orientation_by_Karen_Ahlnas.pdf

Always ask when in doubt. There should be no threshold for asking.

Labeling reagents, solutions, and samples

No chemicals, powders, solutions, reagents, or samples should be left unmarked.

Always label the following information:

name of the substance, solution, reagent, or sample

molarity or percentage

solvent, when applicable

date prepared

your name or initials

Do not write directly on glassware. Use paper tape for writing.

Even Eppendorf tubes or Falcon tubes containing water for balancing the centrifuge must be labeled directly on the tube.

Shared items and equipments

Keep in mind that multiple people use the same materials, resources, and equipment.

Always return items to their correct places after use to ensure also others can find the items.

Empty items from the cell culture room should be returned to the wet lab room, to the coordinator's bench. The lab coordinator will refill them when they are returned there.

Please order so that others also have enough resources for their purposes. See the table under that serves as a general guideline.

Working with Human-Derived Material

When working with human-derived material, such as patient samples, the Oncosys-OVA confidentiality agreement must be signed before handling any samples. Ask the Lab coordinator or Clinical team coordinator for help with this.

Remember that human-derived material always carries a potential risk of infection. Wear a lab coat and gloves, and disinfect surfaces and labware after finishing your work using Virkon.

Be extra careful when using scalpels, as they are very sharp. Discard scalpels and syringe needles in the yellow sharp waste containers.

Hepatitis B vaccination is recommended when working with fresh patient samples. It can be obtained through occupational healthcare at Mehiläinen and should be compensated by your employer. People working on grants should discuss this with their supervisor.

Cell Culture Rules

Biological waste disposal

Biological waste must be placed in the designated biological waste containers. Biological waste includes all materials that have been in contact with cells, cell culture reagents, chemicals, and disposable plasticware that has been in direct contact with cells or reagents.

For example, empty tip boxes are not considered biological waste unless they are contaminated, such as after a spill. Used pipette tips are always considered biological waste.

All non-biological waste in the cell culture room can be placed in the regular waste bin under the sink.

Laminar hoods

Book the hood using the calendar on each laminar hood.

In general, try to use our own laminar hood. If needed, you can book other hoods.

When using another lab’s laminar hood, take our lab’s disposables with you, such as tips and tubes. After finishing your work, take all our lab materials out of the hood. Always leave the hood in the same or better condition than when you started.

When you finish working in the hood:

Clean the hood after use.

Refill the disposables if something has run out. It is good practise to turn off the laminar hood light when not in use.

Rinse the aspirator tube with EtOH, and close the pump.

Put the pipette boy for charging within the hood. Turn on the electricity to the socket in the laminar hood screen if it is off.

Turn off the laminar hood light.

If you are the last user of the day in the laminar hood:

Close the hood.

If you are the last person working in the cell culture room:

Turn off the water bath.

Turn off the big centrifuge.

Turn off the lights.

Laboratory Responsibilities: Coordinator vs. Researcher

If the cell culture incubators are alarming and the lab coordinator is unavailable:

CO₂ level low: Notify lab coordinator immediately. If not available, follow these steps:

Use the lab's phone (stored on coordinators' (Aino/Anastasia) office desk) and call Are's emergency service number 020 530 5700

Ask them to come and replace the CO₂ tank immediately. Tell them that you are calling from Biomedicum 1 (address: Haartmaninkatu 8) and the cell culture room in question is on the 3rd floor, B-wing, room B324a1.

If the personnel of Are ask, our CO₂ tanks are stored on the 2nd floor.

Water level low:

Take a 1–2 liter bottle from the equipment maintenance room and fill it with mQ water.

Refill the water reservoir at the bottom of the cell culture incubator with 1–2 liters of water.

Wet lab resources

Lab coats

Lab coats can be ordered from Lindström Oy https://lindstromgroup.com/in/elindstrom/

The coordinator will handle ordering new lab coats, but researchers are responsible for sending them for washing when needed.

Dirty lab coats can be sent for washing by placing them in Lindström's containers located next to the service elevators on the 3rd floor. New and washed lab coats will return to the same location, but they will be hung on the coat hanger.

Dirty lab coats should be left by Wednesday for washing, and they will be returned the following Thursday.

Liquid nitrogen (LN)

LN usage requires training and key access to the rooms. Training is held on the first Wednesday of every month from 9-10. More details and enrollment by email to Property Office <property.office@biomedicum.fi>

LN storage tank at Haartman. Training and key access can be requested from LTDK laboratorioinsinöörit <ltdk-labengineers@helsinki.fi>.

Wet lab tour

Will be given by the lab coordinator. The tour includes:

Färkkilä wet lab

Cell culture room

Cold storages:

Cold room

-20 freezers in scanner room

-80 freezer in the corridor

back-up -80 freezer in p-floor

Fume hoods: when and how to work there

Equipment maintenance (Välinehuolto in Finnish):

personal/common-use reusables

sterile/non-sterile plastic and glassware

mQ-water

ice

Dry ice and liquid nitrogen (LN).

Wastes and sorting:

Cell culture waste:

Biological waste

Sharp and glass waste

Liquid wastes

Chemical wastes

Not contaminated metal, glass, plastic, cardboard, and paper waste
